name: Altos de Urkiola y Muniketa — circular by the Urkiola Natural Park
name_original: Altos de Urkiola y Muniketa — circular por el Parque Natural de Urkiola
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Amorebieta-Etxano traverse a diverse landscape characterized by rugged mountains, the Ibaizabal River valley, and undulating terrain. The region features a continuous mix of climbs and descents, providing varied experiences for cyclists. To the south, the terrain rises sharply towards the Urkiola Natural Park, offering routes through a setting surrounded by vegetation. Elevations range from the river valley to hills like Mount Bizkargi (563 meters) and higher peaks exceeding 700 meters. Best road cycling routes around Amorebieta-Etxano The most popular road cycling route is Altos de Urkiola y Muniketa — circular by the Urkiola Natural Park, a 41.5 miles (66.8 km) difficult trail that takes 3 hours 34 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation gain through the Urkiola Natural Park. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Arteako Benta – Alto de Sarasola loop from Amorebieta Geralekua, a moderate 32.6 miles (52.5 km) path. This route includes a substantial ascent, offering views of the surrounding landscape. Local road cyclists also love the Puerto de Morga – Climb From Goikoeleixalde loop from Amorebieta Geralekua, a 37.7 miles (60.7 km) trail leading through varied terrain with notable climbs, often completed in about 2 hours 57 minutes. Road cycling around Amorebieta-Etxano is defined by rugged mountains, the Ibaizabal River valley, and the natural beauty of Urkiola Natural Park. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to challenging climbs. The routes in Amorebieta-Etxano are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 10 reviews. Nearly 2000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Amorebieta-Etxano's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Amorebieta-Etxano?
A: There are 14 road cycling routes around Amorebieta-Etxano, offering a diverse range of experiences. These include 1 easy route, 8 moderate routes, and 5 difficult routes, catering to various fitness levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Amorebieta-Etxano?
A: Road cycling in Amorebieta-Etxano is characterized by a continuous mix of climbs, descents, and undulating roads, often referred to as 'Bilbao flat' terrain. You'll encounter rugged mountains, the scenic Ibaizabal River valley, and routes that rise sharply towards the Urkiola Natural Park, providing varied and challenging experiences.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Amorebieta-Etxano?
A: The Basque Country generally offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are often ideal, with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can be warm, while winter might bring cooler, wetter weather, though many routes remain accessible.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier ride?
A: Yes, while many routes feature significant climbs, there is at least one easy road cycling route available in Amorebieta-Etxano. These routes typically offer shorter distances and less elevation gain, providing a gentler introduction to the region's cycling landscape.

Q: Are there challenging climbs for advanced road cyclists?
A: Absolutely. Amorebieta-Etxano is known for its challenging mountainous terrain. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1031517400/altos-de-urkiola-y-muniketa-circular-by-the-urkiola-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Altos de Urkiola y Muniketa — circular by the Urkiola Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; feature significant elevation gains, with some routes exceeding 1000 meters of ascent, demanding very good fitness. The region's rugged mountains provide ample opportunities for demanding climbs.

Q: Can I find long-distance road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, several routes offer longer distances for endurance cyclists. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31614885" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Dima – Dima Village and Scenic Views loop from Amorebieta&lt;/a&gt; covers over 80 km, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39563027" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Morga – View of Laga Beach loop from Amorebieta Geralekua&lt;/a&gt; is also over 80 km, providing extensive rides through varied landscapes.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes?
A: Many of the road cycling routes in Amorebieta-Etxano are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1031517400/altos-de-urkiola-y-muniketa-circular-by-the-urkiola-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Altos de Urkiola y Muniketa — circular by the Urkiola Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39565669" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arteako Benta – Alto de Sarasola loop from Amorebieta Geralekua&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What points of interest or landmarks can I see along road cycling routes?
A: The routes often pass by significant natural and historical sites. You might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/628313" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Urkiola Sanctuary&lt;/a&gt; within the Urkiola Natural Park, or enjoy panoramic views from mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5335984" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Muniketa (Montecalvo)&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5460317" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Sarasola&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features historical sites like the Monastery of Santa María de Zenarruza and the church of Santa María de la Asunción along the Ibaizabal River.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about the routes in Amorebieta-Etxano?
A: The road cycling routes in Amorebieta-Etxano are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 10 reviews. Cyclists frequently praise the diverse terrain, the continuous mix of climbs and descents, and the beautiful natural settings, especially those traversing the Urkiola Natural Park.

Q: Is parking available for cyclists near the routes in Amorebieta-Etxano?
A: Amorebieta-Etxano, as a central municipality, offers various parking options. Many routes, such as those starting 'from Amorebieta Geralekua,' imply convenient access from the town center where parking facilities would typically be available for visitors.

Q: How can I reach the road cycling routes using public transport?
A: Amorebieta-Etxano is well-connected within the Basque Country. Public transport options, including local buses and trains, can provide access to the town center, from which many road cycling routes begin. It's advisable to check local transport schedules and bicycle carriage policies beforehand.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling options in Amorebieta-Etxano?
A: While many routes involve significant elevation, there are easier options available. For family-friendly cycling, look for the designated easy routes which typically have less challenging terrain and shorter distances, often found along flatter sections like those near the Ibaizabal River.

Q: Are there places to stop for refreshments along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, many routes pass through or near small villages and towns where you can find cafes, bars, and restaurants for refreshments. The region's cultural richness means you'll often find opportunities to stop and refuel, especially in settlements like Mañaria or near the Urkiola Sanctuary.
tips: Dima is the perfect place to make a stop and recharge your batteries with a delicious pintxo and coffee, or simply enjoy the beautiful views and the local atmosphere.
Long and very stretched port, little traffic, one of my favorites.
Demanding port from Durango, with beautiful horseshoe curves

The Urkiola Natural Park occupies a large part of the surface of this small town in Durangaldea. Mañaria is surrounded by a splendid landscape, sheltered by beautiful limestone peaks such as Errialtabaso (1,018 m), Mugarra (964 m), Saibi (946 m) and Untzillatx (934 m).

Montecalvo is located at an altitude of 381 meters, offering impressive views of the sea and Urdaibai on one side and Duranguesado on the other. The Muniketa road was the main communication route south through Durango until 1848, when the Autzagane road was improved.
